rulings.Case Title: AL SHAWA VS. JACK TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 9. PLAINTIFF AL SHAWA'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Third Amended Complaint IS GRANTED, ALLOWING 20/20 FASHIONS INC. TO SUBSTITUTE AS PLAINTIFF AND TO FILE A THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE RESCISSION CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 5536.22 MAY NOT BE A PART OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT BECAUSE THERE IS NO PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION UNDER THAT SECTION. THE COURT IS NOT PRECLUDING PLAINTIFF FROM SEEKING RESCISSION ON SOME OTHER BASIS, IF THERE ARE FACTS SUPPORTING SUCH A CLAIM. =(302/PJM/PB)

CROSS-DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE: HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS WHETHER THE COURT SHOULD SEVER THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AND TRANSFER ONLY THE CROSS-COMPLAINT TO MARIN. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES FRANKLIN HOOVER VS. JOAN BORUCKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 13. PETITIONER JAMES HOOVER'S MOTION For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus IS DENIED. THE PRESUMPTION IN VEHICLE CODE SECTION 23152(B) APPLIES GIVEN THAT THE TEST WAS TAKEN WITHIN SEVENTY-ONE MINUTES OF THE ACCIDENT AND ARREST. BELL V D.M.V. (1992) 11 CAL APP 4TH 304, 311. ALTHOUGH PETITIONER PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF PROBLEMS WITH THE MACHINE, THE TESTIMONY OF PETITIONER'S EXPERT CONFIRMS THAT, AT OR ABOUT THE TIME OF PETITIONER'S TEST, THE MACHINE WAS OPERATING WITHIN THE ACCEPTABLE RANGE. TO THE EXTENT IT WAS HIGH BY 0.01, PETITIONER'S TEST STILL EXCEEDED THE LEGAL RATE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CAROL WANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition To Compel Mediation
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 14. PETITIONER CAROL WANG'S Petition To Compel Mediation IS GRANTED. THE ACTION IS STAYED FOR SIXTY DAYS WHILE THE PARTIES MEDIATE THE DISPUTE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT REQUIRING MEDIATION THROUGH A.A.A. IF THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO AGREE UPON A MEDIATOR. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SN SANDS CORPORATION DBA S&S TRUCKING VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 15. PETITIONER SANDS CORP.'S, D.B.A S&S TRUCKING, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE: HEARING REQUIRED. If the Court determines for any reason that the Board's approval was not required, is there any reason why the writ should not issue? Must the Court find that the City's determination that "it may be required to spend more than $10 million was 'entirely lacking in evidentiary support'" to grant the writ? Why/why not? Why should/should not the Court give significant weight to the cost estimates of the Budget Analyst? Do the provisions of the Charter control over the language of Resolution 171-08? The Declaration of Bill Jones states: "OCA's current approach to implement Section 9.118 ¿ is to submit contracts to the Board of Supervisors if there is a reasonable possibility that the contract will require the City to expend more than $10 million." · Apart from this statement and the five attachments, what evidence is there of this approach? · Does the record reflect how long this approach has been ongoing and/or how many contracts have been submitted under this approach? · The five referenced contracts do not appear to be new contracts but extensions of existing contracts as they approached the $10 million level? If so, why is this of any consequence? · What is meant by a reasonable possibility? Who makes the determination? · Is "an anticipated expenditure" the same as a "reasonable possibility"? · Has it been applied to every contract, which if renewed regularly, would result in a contract for longer than 10 years or $10 million? Because submission of the contract to the Board was not in the notice of conditional award, is the City estopped/not estopped to rely on the Board's decision? Is the fact that the Sunset Scavenger cost is higher of any relevance given that the stated purpose of Resolution 171-05 is to ensure the protections of Section 9.118, that is fiscal responsibility? Is the reasonable interpre
